patterns.whatChanged: Hero too standard — text-only hero with just copper rule lacks immersion
patterns.original: Large serif headline + copper rule + basic subtext
patterns.revised: User wants: more visual depth, layered elements, closer to griflan's hero with device/image element, smaller headline, more editorial craft
patterns.pattern: Text-only heroes feel generic for agency sites. Always include a STRUCTURAL visual element for immersion.

patterns.whatChanged: Still not immersive — gradient placeholder divs instead of real images, background too bland
patterns.original: Dark surface cards with gradient placeholders, subtle gradient mesh bg
patterns.revised: User wants REAL IMAGES, not gradient placeholders. Background needs more character. Use design-decisions.md Section 10 hero recipes.
patterns.pattern: Gradient placeholder divs look fake. Always use real images (Unsplash, picsum, etc). Hero MUST use one of the 5 spatial recipes from Section 10.

patterns.whatChanged: PROJECT KILLED. 3 hero iterations failed. Stock photo of color swatches felt generic, back-type + grain + vignette felt overengineered.
patterns.original: LayeredPlanes recipe with stock Unsplash image + ghost FORGE text + grain/vignette
patterns.revised: The real problem: stacking CSS techniques ≠ good design. Griflan works because of CONFIDENT SIMPLICITY — excellent typography, relevant imagery (their actual work, not stock), clean dark space. Overengineering with multiple layers looks like a tech demo, not a premium site. Next time: build the simplest possible hero that's beautiful, then add ONE distinctive element.
